2604.08479.title: AI generates well-liked but templatic empathic responses
2604.08479.paper_summary: Recent research shows that greater numbers of people are turning to Large Language Models (LLMs) for emotional support, and that people rate LLM responses as more empathic than human-written responses. We suggest a reason for this success: LLMs have learned and consistently deploy a well-liked template for expressing empathy. We develop a taxonomy of 10 empathic language "tactics" that include validating someone's feelings and paraphrasing, and apply this taxonomy to characterize the language that people and LLMs produce when writing empathic responses. Across a set of 2 studies comparing a total of n = 3,265 AI-generated (by six models) and n = 1,290 human-written responses, we find that LLM responses are highly formulaic at a discourse functional level. We discovered a template -- a structured sequence of tactics -- that matches between 83--90% of LLM responses (and 60--83\% in a held out sample), and when those are matched, covers 81--92% of the response. By contrast, human-written responses are more diverse. We end with a discussion of implications for the future of AI-generated empathy.
2604.08479.paper_summary_zh: 最近的研究顯示，越來越多的人轉向大型語言模型（LLMs）尋求情感支持，並且人們評價LLM的回應比人類撰寫的回應更具同理心。我們提出這一成功的原因：LLMs已經學會並持續運用一種受歡迎的同理心表達模板。我們開發了一個包含10種同理心語言“策略”的分類法，這些策略包括驗證某人的感受和意譯，並將此分類法應用於描述人類和LLMs在撰寫同理心回應時所產生的語言。在一組包含2項研究的數據中，總共比較了n = 3,265個AI生成的（由六個模型生成）和n = 1,290個人類撰寫的回應，我們發現LLM的回應在話語功能層面上高度公式化。我們發現了一個模板——一個結構化的策略序列——這個模板與83%到90%的LLM回應相匹配（在保留樣本中為60%到83%），並且當這些回應匹配時，覆蓋了81%到92%的回應。相比之下，人類撰寫的回應則更加多樣化。我們最後討論了對AI生成的同理心未來的影響。
2604.08479.author: Emma Gueorguieva et.al.
2604.08479.authors: Emma Gueorguieva, Hongli Zhan, Jina Suh, Javier Hernandez, Tatiana Lau, Junyi Jessy Li, Desmond C. Ong
